verification.target_vocab_notes: 鳴く appears in ている form (鳴いている) showing birds singing
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Contains ている continuous form (鳴いている) which is N4+ grammar. Also contains そうに (楽しそうに - looking happy) which is N4 appearance/seeming grammar
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: N4 ている and そうに constructions (8/10). Vocabulary: Multiple words (6/10). Length: Medium-long (6/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: N4 level
verification.overall_notes: Contains multiple N4+ grammar patterns (ている and そうに). Not suitable for N5

verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: N4 ている form with contraction (7/10). Vocabulary: Basic words (5/10). Length: Short-medium (4/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: N4 level
verification.overall_notes: Contains N4+ ている form. Not suitable for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 書く appears in te-form 書いて (write) with ください
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: negative ず-form (忘れず - without forgetting), に particle, を particle, te-form (書いて) + ください (please)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: ず-form + に construction (6/10). Vocabulary: 忘れる, 手紙 (5/10). Length: Medium (5/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid-upper N5
verification.overall_notes: ず-form (忘れず) is borderline advanced for N5. While technically N5, it's challenging for beginners

verification.target_vocab_notes: 毎年 (まいとし reading) appears at the beginning as time expression
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence. Uses まいとし reading
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: time expression (毎年), ます-form (行きます)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Simple present tense (2/10). Vocabulary: Basic time and verb (2/10). Length: Very short (2/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Beginner N5
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example

verification.target_vocab_notes: 毎年 (まいとし reading) appears at the beginning
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence. Uses まいとし reading
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: time expression, ここ (here), に particle, negative -なきゃ (contraction of -なければ - must). While contracted, this is borderline acceptable for N5
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: なきゃ contraction for obligation (6/10). Vocabulary: Basic words (4/10). Length: Short (3/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid-upper N5
verification.overall_notes: なきゃ is a casual contraction of なければならない. While common, it's borderline advanced for N5

verification.target_vocab_notes: 毎年 (まいとし reading) appears at the beginning with comma
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence. Uses まいとし reading
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: time expression, の (possessive), は particle (doubled for topic shift), に particle (time), い-adjective (暖かい), になる (become)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: になる construction with time/topic structure (7/10). Vocabulary: 春, 暖かい, 天気 (6/10). Length: Long (7/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Advanced N5
verification.overall_notes: Advanced N5 example with complex sentence structure. Uses only N5 grammar but challenging

verification.target_vocab_notes: 明日 (あした) appears at the end with また (again)
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: それじゃ (casual 'well then'), また (again), time expression
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: Fixed casual expression (3/10). Vocabulary: Common phrase (2/10). Length: Very short (2/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Beginner N5
verification.overall_notes: Excellent simple N5 example showing common farewell expression

verification.target_vocab_notes: 明日 (あした) appears as topic with は particle
verification.kana_notes: Perfect match between kana and Japanese sentence
verification.grammar_notes: Uses N5 grammar: たぶん (probably), は particle, が particle, dictionary form (降る), だろう (probably/presumptive)
verification.difficulty_notes: Grammar: だろう presumptive form (5/10). Vocabulary: たぶん, 雪, 降る (5/10). Length: Medium (4/10). Cultural: Universal (2/10). Overall: Mid N5
verification.overall_notes: Good N5 example showing だろう presumptive form for predictions
